title: The Black Pirate

evidence.note: Published 1926; the 95-year US copyright term has expired for works published through 1930, so the film print is public domain in the US by term expiry.

watch.label: Internet Archive — The Black Pirate (1926), full film

editorial: The Black Pirate is a 1926 American silent swashbuckler directed by Albert Parker and produced by and starring Douglas Fairbanks, released through United Artists. One of the first feature films shot entirely in two-color Technicolor, its pioneering use of color and athletic set pieces made it a landmark of silent-era spectacle. Published in 1926, the film print's 95-year US copyright term has expired, placing the photoplay in the public domain by term expiry.
